data.title: Rivian's stock rises post Q4 beat, reporting a $1.28B revenue & an adjusted loss of 54 cents - exceeding expectations.
data.description: Rivian Automotive, Inc. (Nasdaq: RIVN) saw a rise in its stock trading following the announcement of better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results. The company reported an adjusted loss of 54 cents per share, outperforming the expected 68-cent loss. Revenue stood at $1.28 billion, exceeding the forecasted $1.27 billion. Benchmark analyst Mickey Legg maintained a Buy rating on the stock and increased the price forecast from $18 to $25, citing the company's significant progress in the latest quarter. Rivian's return to positive gross profit, aided by Software & Services growth, helped mitigate fluctuations in the core auto business before the R2 launch. Despite ongoing challenges in automotive margins, positive gross profit for the entire year is attributed to enhanced cost control and execution. This article can be read in full on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Chemed Corp. announces $300 million for stock repurchase & 60 cent dividend, financed by operations & credit facility.
data.description: On February 13, 2026, in Cincinnati, it was reported that Chemed Corporation (NYSE: CHE) has received approval from its Board of Directors to allocate an extra $300 million towards purchasing stocks through its current share repurchase initiative. The funding for these repurchases will come from a mix of operational cash flow and tapping into its revolving credit facility.

data.title: Proem Acquisition Corp I closes $130 million IPO with 13,000,000 units sold at $10 each on Feb. 13, 2026.
data.description: Dallas, TX - On February 13, 2026, Proem Acquisition Corp I disclosed the completion of its IPO, issuing a total of 13,000,000 units priced at $10.00 each. This resulted in earning $130,000,000 in total gross proceeds for the company, prior to subtracting underwriting discounts, commissions, and other related expenses.

data.title: Binance reportedly fired 5 compliance investigators who found $1B Iran-linked transactions, potentially violating sanctions laws.
data.description: Binance fired at least five compliance investigators who discovered evidence that Iran-linked entities had received over $1 billion through the exchange, potentially violating sanctions laws. The transactions were made using Tether on the Tron blockchain between March 2024 and August 2025. Three of the investigators came from law enforcement backgrounds in Europe and Asia. In addition to the firings, at least four other top compliance staff have left or been pushed out in the past three months. The exact reasons for the actions are unknown.

data.title: Playmates Toys to launch "Dungeon Crawler Carl" collectibles at Toy Fair 2026, including 6-inch figures and blind box items.
data.description: This summer, Playmates Toys, operating under the brand 'Borant Corporation,' is set to introduce an exclusive collection inspired by Matt Dinniman's highly acclaimed LitRPG series. The lineup will include six-inch figures, blind box collectibles, plush toys, and more. The debut line of Dungeon Crawler Carl merchandise is scheduled to be revealed in New York on February 13, 2026.

data.title: Binance France CEO survives home invasion; attackers arrested.
data.description: The head of Binance France was the victim of a home invasion, yet managed to escape unscathed. Fortunately, the perpetrators were apprehended following the incident.

data.title: $3.85M in Ethereum from Mixin Network breach found its way to Tornado Cash, post 2 years. The total heist stood at $200M in 2023.
data.description: Following the $200 million exploit of Mixin in 2023, wallets involved in the incident recently reactivated after being dormant for almost two years. These wallets transferred the funds to coin mixer Tornado Cash.

data.title: Coinbase's stock jumps despite Q4 underperformance, deemed undervalued by analysts.
data.description: Coinbase recently reported financial results that slightly missed Wall Street estimates. Despite this, Bernstein noted that investors are downplaying the exchange's ability to withstand challenges.

data.title: Anchorage, Kamino enable borrowing against SOL in custody without moving assets, amid DeFi regulation talks.
data.description: As discussions continue among US lawmakers regarding oversight of decentralized finance (DeFi), institutions now have the opportunity to borrow against staked SOL within a new framework. This innovative approach enables institutions to retain their assets in qualified custody while utilizing them as collateral for borrowing. This development comes at a time when the value of SOL and other cryptocurrencies is on the rise, with SOL currently trading at approximately $150 per token. The integration of staked SOL into borrowing practices provides a new avenue for institutions to leverage their crypto holdings, potentially influencing the broader DeFi landscape.

data.title: Bitcoin could hit $72K if V-shaped recovery finishes; $69K post-CPI rally suggests $60K was sell-off bottom.
data.description: Following a significant post-CPI rally, Bitcoin has surged above $69,000. This increase in value could potentially contribute to completing the V-shaped recovery chart pattern. Moreover, it may serve as confirmation that the sell-off bottom was at $60,000.
